decision_question: Should product teams adopt a governed business-readiness gate before coding agents begin MVP implementation?
initiating_context: Teams can generate code faster than they can determine whether a proposed product is worth building.

target_user.description: Leaders coordinating business discovery with coding-agent execution.
target_user.usage_context: Before committing an agentic engineering budget to an MVP.

economic_buyer.description: Product or innovation leader accountable for delivery budget.
economic_buyer.value_recipient: The team avoids low-value builds and gets an auditable handoff into implementation.

job_to_be_done: Decide whether an MVP is the cheapest sufficient experiment and bound what a coding agent may implement.
observed_problem: Business evidence, product scope, and implementation authority are usually scattered across chat, documents, and CI.
current_alternatives: Allowing the coding agent to infer permission
expected_value: Fewer premature builds, faster review, and a reproducible audit trail from evidence to bounded action.
strategic_context: Agentic development makes decision quality and authorization clarity a first-order productivity constraint.

constraints.description: The judge path must run without credentials or network access.
